Exploratory Data Analysis: Socio-Economic Predictors of Malaria

Applied Statistics Portfolio Project

2026-04-17

  • Introduction
  • Methodology
  • Results

Introduction

This report performs exploratory data analysis (EDA) for a malaria epidemiology dataset, with a focus on socio-economic predictors. The objective is to characterize variable distributions, assess data quality, and visualize relationships that motivate multivariable logistic regression.

knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE, fig.width = 9, fig.height = 6, dpi = 300)

Methodology

Load Packages and Data

required_packages <- c(
  "tidyverse",
  "janitor",
  "GGally",
  "scales"
)

missing_packages <- required_packages[!(required_packages %in% installed.packages()[, "Package"])]
if (length(missing_packages) > 0) {
  install.packages(missing_packages, dependencies = TRUE)
}

invisible(lapply(required_packages, library, character.only = TRUE))
data_path <- file.path("data", "malaria_data.csv")
output_dir <- "outputs"
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
  dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
}

malaria_data <- readr::read_csv(data_path, show_col_types = FALSE) |>
  janitor::clean_names()

numeric_vars <- malaria_data |>
  dplyr::select(where(is.numeric))

Key Data Objects (Printed Output)

dim(malaria_data)
## [1] 2 9
names(malaria_data)
## [1] "malaria_status"        "age"                   "sex"                  
## [4] "household_income"      "education_years"       "household_size"       
## [7] "bed_net_use"           "distance_to_clinic_km" "housing_quality"
head(malaria_data, 10)
## # A tibble: 2 × 9
##   malaria_status   age sex    household_income education_years household_size
##            <dbl> <dbl> <chr>             <dbl>           <dbl>          <dbl>
## 1              1     7 Female             120.               3              6
## 2              0    32 Male               540               11              4
## # ℹ 3 more variables: bed_net_use <dbl>, distance_to_clinic_km <dbl>,
## #   housing_quality <chr>
summary(malaria_data)
##  malaria_status      age            sex            household_income
##  Min.   :0.00   Min.   : 7.00   Length:2           Min.   :120.5   
##  1st Qu.:0.25   1st Qu.:13.25   Class :character   1st Qu.:225.4   
##  Median :0.50   Median :19.50   Mode  :character   Median :330.2   
##  Mean   :0.50   Mean   :19.50                      Mean   :330.2   
##  3rd Qu.:0.75   3rd Qu.:25.75                      3rd Qu.:435.1   
##  Max.   :1.00   Max.   :32.00                      Max.   :540.0   
##  education_years household_size  bed_net_use   distance_to_clinic_km
##  Min.   : 3      Min.   :4.0    Min.   :0.00   Min.   :2.100        
##  1st Qu.: 5      1st Qu.:4.5    1st Qu.:0.25   1st Qu.:3.875        
##  Median : 7      Median :5.0    Median :0.50   Median :5.650        
##  Mean   : 7      Mean   :5.0    Mean   :0.50   Mean   :5.650        
##  3rd Qu.: 9      3rd Qu.:5.5    3rd Qu.:0.75   3rd Qu.:7.425        
##  Max.   :11      Max.   :6.0    Max.   :1.00   Max.   :9.200        
##  housing_quality   
##  Length:2          
##  Class :character  
##  Mode  :character  
##                    
##                    
## 
missingness_table <- malaria_data |>
  summarise(across(everything(), ~ sum(is.na(.)))) |>
  tidyr::pivot_longer(cols = everything(), names_to = "variable", values_to = "missing_count") |>
  arrange(desc(missing_count))

missingness_table
## # A tibble: 9 × 2
##   variable              missing_count
##   <chr>                         <int>
## 1 malaria_status                    0
## 2 age                               0
## 3 sex                               0
## 4 household_income                  0
## 5 education_years                   0
## 6 household_size                    0
## 7 bed_net_use                       0
## 8 distance_to_clinic_km             0
## 9 housing_quality                   0
if (ncol(numeric_vars) > 1) {
  cor_matrix <- stats::cor(numeric_vars, use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
  cor_matrix
} else {
  cor_matrix <- matrix(NA_real_, nrow = 1, ncol = 1, dimnames = list("insufficient_numeric_columns", "insufficient_numeric_columns"))
  cor_matrix
}
##                       malaria_status age household_income education_years
## malaria_status                     1  -1               -1              -1
## age                               -1   1                1               1
## household_income                  -1   1                1               1
## education_years                   -1   1                1               1
## household_size                     1  -1               -1              -1
## bed_net_use                       -1   1                1               1
## distance_to_clinic_km              1  -1               -1              -1
##                       household_size bed_net_use distance_to_clinic_km
## malaria_status                     1          -1                     1
## age                               -1           1                    -1
## household_income                  -1           1                    -1
## education_years                   -1           1                    -1
## household_size                     1          -1                     1
## bed_net_use                       -1           1                    -1
## distance_to_clinic_km              1          -1                     1

Results

Visualization 1: Distribution of Household Income by Malaria Status

income_plot <- malaria_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(
    malaria_status = factor(
      malaria_status,
      levels = c(0, 1),
      labels = c("Negative", "Positive")
    )
  ) |>
  ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = household_income, fill = malaria_status)) +
  ggplot2::geom_histogram(
    bins = 35,
    alpha = 0.7,
    position = "identity",
    color = "white"
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set1", name = "Malaria Status") +
  ggplot2::scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::label_comma()) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Distribution of Household Income by Malaria Status",
    x = "Household Income",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"))

income_plot

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "eda_income_distribution.png"),
  plot = income_plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)

Visualization 2: Correlation Heatmap of Numeric Variables

cor_df <- as.data.frame(as.table(cor_matrix))
colnames(cor_df) <- c("Var1", "Var2", "Correlation")

correlation_plot <- ggplot2::ggplot(cor_df, ggplot2::aes(Var1, Var2, fill = Correlation)) +
  ggplot2::geom_tile(color = "white") +
  ggplot2::scale_fill_gradient2(
    low = "#2166AC",
    mid = "white",
    high = "#B2182B",
    midpoint = 0,
    limits = c(-1, 1),
    na.value = "grey90"
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Correlation Heatmap of Epidemiological Variables",
    x = NULL,
    y = NULL
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  ggplot2::theme(
    axis.text.x = ggplot2::element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
    plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold")
  )

correlation_plot

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "eda_correlation_heatmap.png"),
  plot = correlation_plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 7,
  dpi = 300
)

Visualization 3: Estimated Malaria Probability Across Education Years

education_plot <- malaria_data |>
  dplyr::mutate(malaria_status = as.numeric(malaria_status)) |>
  ggplot2::ggplot(ggplot2::aes(x = education_years, y = malaria_status)) +
  ggplot2::geom_jitter(
    alpha = 0.15,
    width = 0.2,
    height = 0.04,
    color = "#666666"
  ) +
  ggplot2::geom_smooth(
    method = "glm",
    method.args = list(family = "binomial"),
    se = TRUE,
    color = "#1B7837",
    fill = "#A6DBA0",
    linewidth = 1.1
  ) +
  ggplot2::scale_y_continuous(
    breaks = c(0, 0.25, 0.5, 0.75, 1),
    labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 1)
  ) +
  ggplot2::labs(
    title = "Estimated Malaria Probability Across Education Years",
    x = "Years of Education",
    y = "Predicted Malaria Probability"
  ) +
  ggplot2::theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
  ggplot2::theme(plot.title = ggplot2::element_text(face = "bold"))

education_plot
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

ggplot2::ggsave(
  filename = file.path(output_dir, "eda_malaria_vs_education.png"),
  plot = education_plot,
  width = 9,
  height = 6,
  dpi = 300
)
## `geom_smooth()` using formula = 'y ~ x'

Export Quick EDA Summary

eda_quick_summary <- malaria_data |>
  dplyr::summarise(
    n = dplyr::n(),
    malaria_prevalence = mean(malaria_status, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

eda_quick_summary
## # A tibble: 1 × 2
##       n malaria_prevalence
##   <int>              <dbl>
## 1     2                0.5
readr::write_csv(
  eda_quick_summary,
  file.path(output_dir, "eda_quick_summary.csv")
)
